summary:
Claim: Glycosylation is not a post-transcriptional modification of RNA.  
Evidence: - Post-transcriptional RNA modifications include splicing, 5′ capping, and 3′ polyadenylation—all occurring after transcription to mature mRNA. - Glycosylation involves attaching sugar groups to proteins (e.g., via N-/O-linkage) and has no recognized role in RNA modification.  
Answer: D

Answer: D. Answer choice: D
